windows.embed_text_v2: Stirring molten wax in a tall stainless-steel pouring pitcher with a black spatula at a white counter, three foil-wrapped glass candle jars with centered wicks and graduated beakers nearby, melting stage
windows.summary_v2: A person stirs and blends melted wax in a tall stainless-steel pouring pitcher at a counter set with foil-collared wicked jars and beakers, then reaches for a yellow tool.

tags_v2.action: stirring melted wax in pouring pitcher
tags_v2.on_screen_text: flame/brand logo watermark in top-right corner

seconds_v2.desc: A person (torso only, navy patterned shirt, bare hands, no face) stands at a white counter stirring a tall stainless-steel pouring pitcher with a black-handled spatula; in front sit three foil-wrapped glass jars with wicks and small glass beakers, a flame-logo watermark in the top-right corner.
The hand pushes the black spatula down into the steel melting pitcher, stirring the wax inside; the three aluminium-foil-collared candle jars and a wicked beaker remain lined up on the counter.
Continued stirring of the wax in the brushed-steel pitcher; the other hand rests on the pitcher's black handle while the foil-wrapped jars and graduated beakers stay in the foreground.
The spatula is worked around the inside of the steel container to blend the melted wax, the person's torso centered behind the pitcher, jars with protruding wicks at left and right.
Stirring continues with the black tool inside the tall metal pitcher; an empty glass beaker sits at far right, the three foil-collared jars holding centered wicks in front.
The hand lifts the spatula slightly while still inside the steel pouring pot, mixing the wax; foil-wrapped jars and wick sticks remain arranged on the white countertop.
Both hands near the top of the steel pitcher, one stirring with the spatula, continuing to blend the molten wax before pouring.
The person leans in slightly, still stirring the wax in the brushed-metal pitcher with the black-handled tool, foil jars and beakers staged in front.
One hand now picks up a yellow-handled tool (thermometer/lighter) from beside the pitcher while the other keeps the spatula in the wax, preparing to check the melted wax.
